title: CSS </style> regex misses whitespace variants
description:
LOCATION: src/server.rs:140

FINDING: The regex used to strip </style> from operator-supplied CSS is 'regex::RegexBuilder::new("</style>")' with case_insensitive(true). This matches only the exact sequence </style> (no whitespace between 'style' and '>'). HTML parsers accept </style > (trailing space or any whitespace before the closing >) as a valid closing tag. An operator-supplied CSS file containing that pattern would close the <style> block early, allowing injection of arbitrary HTML nodes into the page body.

IMPACT: If operator-supplied CSS (from theme_tokens_path or custom_css_path) contains </style > — for example, sourced from a third-party CSS library with that text in a comment — an attacker who can influence the CSS file content can escape the <style> block and inject arbitrary HTML. The CSP (default-src 'none') limits JavaScript execution but does not prevent HTML injection (e.g., hidden forms, CSS-based data exfiltration). Fix: change regex to '</style\s*>'.
close_reason: Fixed: CSS regex changed to r"</style\s*>" matching whitespace variants
